en-us.html:
She'll be followed long after she's gone.

Story

I salute and stand en garde as Jeanne takes up position in front of me. There's a determination in her eyes that could easily be mistaken for severity. But I can honestly say I have never faced such intensity, such presence, such poise. And it comes to her naturally, as easily as the air that we breathe. Her bearing is impeccable, her posture excellent. And as she takes a few steps towards me, I step backwards as a reaction to her determination, fortitude and radiance. But I can't let myself give up ground. I strike, and she stops my attack with the flat of her shield. I lunge again, and she parries my strike with the edge of her sword. She shows no hesitation, no fear. Her goal is victory, and she goes all-out to achieve it.

In my last lesson, she had talked to me about her way of seeing things. She believed she was only an instrument, dedicated to completing a mission that was so much bigger than herself. And she believed that all the soldiers of the Aegis needed to adopt this same mindset. There was no room for doubt. We just needed to keep going until this destiny was fulfilled, until the world was free of the Tumult's clutches... The undertaking was a noble one. It had to be our moral compass at all times, our guiding light when the way forward wasn't clear. If Jeanne had led our troops, I know I would have followed her to the end of the world. But it was time for someone else to take her place, to free humanity from its imprisonment. Could I carry the standard? Did I have enough charisma to get everyone to rally around me? Her strike clangs against my shield and I stagger backwards. It's time I proved myself.

blocks.text: She'll be followed long after she's gone.

blocks.title: Story
paragraphs: I salute and stand en garde as Jeanne takes up position in front of me. There's a determination in her eyes that could easily be mistaken for severity. But I can honestly say I have never faced such intensity, such presence, such poise. And it comes to her naturally, as easily as the air that we breathe. Her bearing is impeccable, her posture excellent. And as she takes a few steps towards me, I step backwards as a reaction to her determination, fortitude and radiance. But I can't let myself give up ground. I strike, and she stops my attack with the flat of her shield. I lunge again, and she parries my strike with the edge of her sword. She shows no hesitation, no fear. Her goal is victory, and she goes all-out to achieve it.

In my last lesson, she had talked to me about her way of seeing things. She believed she was only an instrument, dedicated to completing a mission that was so much bigger than herself. And she believed that all the soldiers of the Aegis needed to adopt this same mindset. There was no room for doubt. We just needed to keep going until this destiny was fulfilled, until the world was free of the Tumult's clutches... The undertaking was a noble one. It had to be our moral compass at all times, our guiding light when the way forward wasn't clear. If Jeanne had led our troops, I know I would have followed her to the end of the world. But it was time for someone else to take her place, to free humanity from its imprisonment. Could I carry the standard? Did I have enough charisma to get everyone to rally around me? fr-fr.html:
Elle continuera d'inspirer les foules bien après son départ.

Histoire

Je salue et me mets en garde, alors que Jeanne prend position en face de moi. Il y a de la résolution dans son regard, que l'on pourrait facilement confondre avec de la dureté. Mais en vérité, jamais je n'ai fait face à autant d'intensité, de présence... de prestance, même. Et cette dernière lui vient naturellement, aussi aisément que l'air qu'on respire. Son port est impeccable, son maintien excellent. Et alors qu'elle fait quelques pas dans ma direction, je recule devant sa détermination, pleine de force d'âme et de radiance. Mais je ne peux me permettre de céder du terrain. Je frappe, et elle cueille mon assaut du plat de son bouclier. Je me fends à nouveau, et elle pare mon coup du tranchant de sa lame. Il n'y a aucune hésitation, aucune crainte. Son objectif est la victoire, et elle se dirigera vers elle tous azimuts.

Durant la dernière leçon, elle m'avait parlé de sa façon de voir les choses. Elle n'était qu'un instrument, dédié à l'accomplissement d'une mission qui la transcendait. Et pour elle, tous les soldats de l'Aegis se devaient d'être dans ce cas de figure. Il n'y avait pas à douter, juste à avancer jusqu'à ce que cette destinée se réalise, jusqu'à libérer le monde du joug du Tumulte... L'entreprise était noble. Elle devait en tout temps rester notre compas moral, notre boussole inflexible quand le chemin n'était pas clair. Si Jeanne avait été à la tête de nos troupes, je sais que je l'aurais suivie jusqu'au bout du monde... Mais il était temps que d'autres prennent cette place, pour mener l'humanité hors de son enfermement... Pouvais-je être cet étendard ? Avais-je le charisme suffisant pour que tous se rallient à moi ? Son coup résonne contre mon bouclier et je titube en arrière. Il est temps de le prouver.

blocks.text: Elle continuera d'inspirer les foules bien après son départ.
